brief.title: aion-awl: complete the parser + printer from the near-done branch (one failing round-trip golden)
brief.objective: Finish AWL-2 starting from the existing branch dev/AWL-2 (local, in this repo): fix the single failing golden test research_report_normalizes_to_golden_and_preserves_comments — the printer/parser currently DROPS a trailing comment — and deliver the full AWL-2 acceptance with all gates green.
brief.context: FIRST ACTION: in your provisioned workspace run `git merge --no-edit dev/AWL-2` — that branch contains a near-complete parser (src/parser.rs), canonical printer (src/printer.rs), extended lexer trivia support, both canonical fixtures, and tests/parser_printer.rs (150 lines). It is clippy-clean and 15 of 16 tests pass. The ONE failure: research_report_normalizes_to_golden_and_preserves_comments in tests/parser_printer.rs — the canonical output is missing the trailing comment 'rebinds — every later step sees the revised draft' (it sits on the `as draft` line of the revise step in tests/fixtures/research_report.awl) and spans drift after that point. Diagnose where the trailing-comment trivia on step-field lines (specifically `as`) is lost — attachment in the parser, or emission in the printer — and fix it at that seam. Then re-verify the whole AWL-2 contract (below). The grammar contract is docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-0-SPEC-DRAFT.md; canonical comment rendering: own-line comments print on their own line at the indentation of the item they precede; trailing comments print on the same line separated by exactly two spaces. PRINTER DISCIPLINE (non-negotiable): print renders purely from the AST + attached trivia; the Document must not retain raw source text or source slices for re-emission.
scope_out: Do NOT modify tests/fixtures/research_report.awl or tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.awl (the source fixtures) — only their .canonical.awl goldens may be regenerated, and only such that every fixture comment appears in them

Do NOT weaken, delete, or #[ignore] any existing test; the failing golden test must pass by fixing the code, not the assertion — except where an assertion is provably wrong against the spec, in which case say so explicitly in your report
No changes to any other crate, no external dependencies (std only)
Do not modify the spec or sketch documents
acceptance: git history shows the work builds on dev/AWL-2 (merge or cherry-picks), not a from-scratch rewrite

research_report_normalizes_to_golden_and_preserves_comments passes: print(parse(research_report.awl)) contains EVERY comment from the fixture, including the trailing comment on the revise step's `as draft` line, and matches the committed canonical golden byte-for-byte
The canonical goldens round-trip: print(parse(canonical)) == canonical byte-identical, for both fixtures

Anti-echo holds: the Document stores no source text or slice references for re-emission; a test mutates the AST in code and sees the mutation in print output; two differently-formatted sources of the same document print identically
Printer idempotence on both fixtures plus non-canonical whitespace variants: print(parse(print(parse(src)))) == print(parse(src))

Parse errors carry correct spans, covered by tests (two world-touching fields in one step, finish missing, call outside do, unknown step field, unterminated record construction, wrong handler-block indentation)
Every test in the crate passes: cargo test -p aion-awl fully green; clippy -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all leaves the tree unchanged
brief.notes: This is a completion brief, not a rewrite: the diff you add on top of dev/AWL-2 should be small and targeted at the trivia-attachment defect plus anything the full acceptance re-check surfaces. The reviewers will see the whole crate diff vs main — that is intended; this code has not yet had its adversarial review.
